messages.content: Read the entire spec and then look at the spec wearing that sigil.

Wearing this sigil, the spec mostly holds its own shape. The strongest through-line is the recursive method itself: narrate, notice language, decompose, bind invariants, measure coherence, test coverage against vision. The imported ontologies give it a real semantic spine instead of metaphor floating by itself.

What I see most clearly is that the editor side is much more collapsed than the design-partner side. The surfaces for writing, navigation, storage, and basic editing feel projectable, while Coverage and CoherenceMetric still read like powerful but partly ungrounded centers of gravity. They know what they must mean, but not yet enough about how the partner experiences tracing, surfacing, and reporting without inventing.

Wearing the sigil also makes one gap stand out: the root vision says convergence is measured over time until a coding agent can project spec to code, but the path from Coverage complete to actual projection remains thin. So the spec can currently express “what good specification feels like” more precisely than “how the system knows it is ready to hand off.”
